review_title: Simple and Outbox IT Infrastructure Monitoring Tool
author: Prashant D.
pros: Nagios provide a centralized view of system status and provide alerts when issue arise over mail and sms.It supports multiple plugins and extensions with the help of plugins we can monitor server, application, databases, network devices and store their trends. It can also escalate alerts to multiple users or groups based on specific criteria.
cons: Not facing any issue yet,easy to use and maintain.

review_title: Monitoring Large scale Network at a glance.
description: our large scale network and different network devices we are unable to monitor and resolve network based problems timely and devices are increase time by time. due to avoid network failure and need notifications of downtime we study and installed Nagios XI and other solution in our Infrastructure.
author: Irfan A.
pros: Open source and easy to configure easy to customized Notification/Alert in case of any failure. work on SNMP Protocol free add-ons to download and develop your customization
cons: License cost is very high Interface is create confusing ( in Free Version ) Issue in monitoring in network throughput (bandwidth user wise ) Only monitoring tool , configuration are not allowed.

review_title: Nagios XI: capable, flexible, but occasionally frustrating
description: Nagios XI has stood us in very good stead at both my current and former employer - just about 20 years now - where teams I was part of leveraged it extensively for AIX and Linux monitoring. The core functionality is pretty much what you expect from a commonplace monitoring platform (e.g. OK/Warn/Crit alert thresholds, configurable polling intervals and notification lists, etc), but with some additional benefits that are quite useful (intelligent use of groups/templates, change management, storing of historical data with excellent interactive graphs). However, the experience is marred by some "quirks," like the way that historical data is stored in RRD files being difficult to maintain (adding and removing data elements like disks or filesystems breaks it), and the fact that email notifications are sent separately per contact (so if you use distribution lists and a person is on more than one of them, they get multiple emails). On the whole, having taken the time to learn our way around Nagios XI and customize it to our needs, it's been enormously useful - but it definitely required that investment to create find workarounds to its little oddities and create our own custom monitoring scripts.
author: Eric G.
pros: Nagios is extremely flexible and easy to implement - a great solution for those who can or need to create a tailor-made monitoring platform.
cons: Nagios XI grew out of the shareware space where it was often used for zero-cost Linux monitoring, and though XI comes with Support and a few more bells and whistles, it still shows its roots. Out of the box monitoring options are not as comprehensive as some other platforms (dropping AIX , and the core functionality of the platform can be rather "quirky" at times.

review_title: The horse and buggy of monitoring
description: Nagios XI is like a horse and buggy: you use it when you'd like a taste of what the old world used to be like. Does it work? Yes, in the same way that a horse and buggy will get you wherever you'd like to go. But if you want to get somewhere in an efficient manner, and you don't want to deal with the tool taking a huge crap every once in a while, use a more modern, metric-based system like Prometheus.
author: Justin S.
pros: People love Nagios because it "just works" with its check scripts, and Nagios XI adds an actually usable UX on top of that basic experience. It includes a small number of "wizards" that can help set up common monitoring configurations.
cons: The Nagios monitoring agents aren't commercially supported, so good luck getting any features changed on those. You need to handle assembling a collection of test scripts, distributing them, and installing their dependencies on all managed nodes. The Nagios model, which mainly focuses on "mutable" infrastructure versus "immutable" infrastructure (Docker containers) is outdated in today's world.

review_title: Nagios XI Basic - One stop solution for monitoring
description: Nagios XI was easy to setup (from the tools we tested), although it has many plugins you can always and easily create your own plugin (anything that is measurable), it has good alerting system when it comes to emails, seems like the GUI/UX could have been more modern but overall experience is very good.
author: Gil B.
pros: I really liked the easy setup and the fact that it's a one stop solution with the amount of available plugins
cons: For certain configuration you need to read the manual doesn't seem to be trivial titles. Also the wizards have good defaults but there should be an easier way for customization
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Excellent Product
description: Good product and value for money. ROI from day one.
author: PAVAN K.
pros: Value for money, easily availablity of pluggins and more stable product
cons: Improved UI and more user friendly console

review_title: A powerful monitoring solution
description: Nagios XI is a powerful server and network monitoring solution. The automation monitoring provides the continuous feedback about the product and infrastructure which help in enhancing the product quality. The advance user interface help in analysis and administration.
author: Vandana G.
pros: Nagios XI provide a complete monitoring solution for any kind of server, application, web-service or network. User interface is easy to understand and simple to customize the layout or design for each user's priority. Nagios XI monitoring host fetch the quick data from nodes and GUI interact the data into the useful information. Configuration wizards simplify the setup process for new nodes monitoring. Integrated nodes trigger the notification if it needs to increase the capacity of the system. Configuration snapshot helps in saving the day in case of new check-in messed it up and you need a immediate rollback to the last working stage.
cons: Schedule Reports are limited to enterprise but in case of Standard edition we need to setup the jobs to generate the schedule reports. Advance reporting should have some bulk server options. Monitoring of sensitive data like certificate expiry should have some simple wizards as configuring the web-service and then monitoring the certificate is a long process such need to be simplified.

review_title: Entire network at your fingertips
description: Great product. Not only I learned something new, I was also able to impress the management with the results of this $0 product.
author: Sergey M.
pros: I implemented a free version of the application to monitor my complex network, spreadout across multiple locations. While it took me several attempts to get it right, once installed, I was pleasanty surprised with what I saw. Having my network distributed across multiple locations, it was hard to monitor the upstate / resources of my servers and other network devices. Once I configure Nagious to my liking, I had every network device that I wanted to monitor on the consolidated map. Very simple configuration of the config files, once you get an idea of how to work them. Really great result for a free product
cons: Being a Linux newbie, it did take me some poking around, before I got it right. You really have to understand the structure of the product and Linux OS itself to get it right. Config files were confusing at first, but once I figured them out, it was a breeze to add / remove hosts.

review_title: The IT monitoring tool for faults detection.
description: Nagios XI is helping us to do the monitoring completely automatic. The advance dashboard and admin dashboard designed so well and easy to configure as per each user need. The scheduled managed report help in to scaling up the capacity planning of the infrastructure. Nagios XI is providing the complete and comprehensive IT infrastructure monitoring to many of our projects and help us to deliver the project faster and more efficient way.
author: Abhinav K.
pros: Nagios XI is a powerful monitoring tool for faults detection which provide the notification via push, email or SMS. Nagios XI allows us to rollback the configuration on the last working stage in case of fault detection. The new wizard is easy and provide multiple option at the time of setup. Nagios XI have an exclusive feature of bulk modification of attributes which help in to monitoring the same set on monitoring module on newly setup environment. Audit trails feature allow to scrutinize the modification or setup.
cons: Mobile interface is newly develop and need more development, it should provide more option for audit log and add more detail for configuration rollback option. Scheduled report should be included in standard edition.

review_title: Nagios XI - The Open Source Enterprise level Network Monitoring Tool.
description: Nagios Xi web interface user friendly. It allows us to keep the eyes on the complete infrastructure run without any bug as it helps us to identify the failures and also identify the cause of the failure. Configuration snapshot is also helpful to clone the configuration monitoring settings.
author: Gurleen K.
pros: Nagios XI is an Enterprise level monitoring tool as it helps in monitoring the service, infrastructure and network. Powerful dashboard provides all the information at the one place which helps us to monitor the infrastructure. Security can be set on the muti-level users, as it allows the client to view the required components for which they are authorized. The GUI is also very user friendly. Customization wizards is also easy and layout can be configured according to the need of the infrastructure.
cons: Master Node is configurable on LINUX platform only. So this makes it necessary to learn LINUX for team that will setup and implement it. Once the slave nodes are 80+ it makes the Nagios XI interface slow. The Mobile interface also needs more enhancements in it. Upgrading the advance version is also complex and pre planning and backups are also required for the cases.

review_title: Eficiente para una monitorización básica, aunque con margen de mejora
description: He utilizado Nagios XI como herramienta de monitorización en un entorno con pocos servidores para supervisar parámetros clave como el uso de CPU, memoria y espacio en disco. Mi experiencia ha sido positiva en términos de fiabilidad y efectividad para tareas básicas, aunque la configuración inicial me llevó algo de tiempo y paciencia.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Me ha gustado su flexibilidad, ya que permite una amplia personalización con plugins y scripts para adaptarse a necesidades específicas. También me gusta su capacidad para definir alertas. Su sistema de notificaciones es fiable y puede integrarse con múltiples canales de comunicación. Permite la opción gratuita para 7 servidores.
cons: La Interfaz de usuario, aunque funcional, su diseño es poco intuitivo y podría mejorarse en términos de usabilidad. La curva de aprendizaje, ya que requiere un nivel técnico medio para configuraciones avanzadas, lo que puede ser un obstáculo para usuarios nuevos.

review_title: A solid network monitor and more...
description: Consistent alerting of network outages and potential server and application problems.
author: Jose V.
pros: We introduced Nagios XI into our network a few years ago to help monitor network performance as well as for usage alerts. It has been a great tool to keep track of just about everything such as network interface traffic, volume sizes and service and process alerts. The web interface is pretty snappy and easy to get around in. The product just does it's job. We were considering Solarwinds NPM at the same time but glad we choose Nagios instead.
cons: You need to a know a little Linux and it's a little complicated at first to set up more advanced alerting. However, with a little time, you can become a pro.

review_title: is easy for use, i use it every day in my work for monitoring a big network with a 1000 users.
description: i can monitor my network in seconds, and i can see the status of the nodes and the clients in the map mode, and if you install nagstamon is a very solid tools for any work in network monitoring.
author: Alejandro M.
pros: it is very easy to install in a server, and very intuitive when using, the interface is really fantastic, and the map mode really show you how is the structure of your network, and the status of your client and your nodes.
cons: the interface is a little slow when you have a many clients in the network, and the search engine is very precise and does not look for possible match.

review_title: Best Monitoring tool for severs
description: Use for server infrastructure like developments using three tier architecture.
author: Ishara M.
pros: I manage server infrastructure of my company using this.
cons: It is free up to 7 nodes.Expand infrastructures get this issue.

review_title: Essentiel dans un service IT
description: Je démarre mon Dashboard tous les matins, et si tout est vert je passe à des taches plus importantes, top !
author: Cyril L.
pros: L’utilisation au quotidien, une fois le tout bien parametré, est un vrai gain de temps, et permet de se concentrer sur d’autre chose que la surveillance. L’info n’est plus à aller chercher, elle vient à vous si besoin.
cons: Comme tous les logiciels paramètrables ou personnalisables, là encore il peut être complexe de trouver, ou adapter des plugin compatible avec votre matériel et votre besoin.

review_title: Very nice tool for network monitoring
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Nagios XI is a very complet tool for monitoring computer networks.
cons: Nagios XI can be quite complex to set up the first time you use it.

review_title: Etude sur Najios
description: Supervision en temps reelles des servers, RAM DISQUE DUR PROCESSEUR Etat actif ou eteint les alertes
author: joel T.
pros: Son interface graphique , simple, agreable, son monitoring reseau
cons: configuration pas facile, prix couteux,

review_title: Monitoring essentials out of the box
description: The experience is positive for the most part could use more plugins and a better way to set users on specific notofication alerts.
author: Gil B.
pros: Unbosrding is fast and easy. Out of the box default monitoring metrics are really what you need to get started, they have a handful of ready to use plugins and in any other case you could build your own custom plugin real easy.
cons: The GUI doesn't seem to be most updated and some of the features are hard to find (UX issue)

review_title: allows us to monitor the network equipment and help us know in real time when a fault occurs
author: Jorge M.
pros: It allows you to monitor many network devices, such as switches, routers, servers, helping to monitor network performance and track traffic on the network interface. very easy to install on a server, the interface is very intuitive, and has a variety of customization options since Nagios is an open source software which makes it easy to add custom scripts to extend the available services.
cons: It is a bit difficult to configure, but once configured works very well, it continues to use add-ons that in some cases work perfectly but others are painful to work, the interface is a bit slow when you have many clients on the network, and the Search is very precise and does not look for possible matches.

review_title: Complete Monitoring system
description: it has been a critical part of our organization and has never let us down.
author: Umair Ali K.
pros: Nagios XI is the only NMS being used in our infrastructure for monitoring LAN, WLAN, WAN, Virtual infrastructure and physical servers. it supports almost all endpoint types and monitoring protocols. it has built in templates for popular endpoints which makes it very easy to add them.
cons: the reporting feature has limited templates and they cannot be edited. you can create new templates but they also have limitations. some reports cannot be run on groups of devices while others show very little information.

review_title: Highly customizable monitoring
author: Michael F.
pros: The ability to extend monitoring to custom monitors
cons: A steep learning curve to use portions of the product

review_title: Nagio XI makes Nagios SO much better
description: The best benefit is the ability to create a monitor in a GUI rather than a command line.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I really like the graphical user interface overlay on top of Nagios Core, it makes it so much easier to create monitors.
cons: The only complaint I have with Nagios XI is not with the software but instead with the way that people do not take ownership for the monitors that they create. The software works great when done right.

review_title: Good steady monitoring tool
author: VINAYAK S.
pros: The tool is simple to use and easy to deploy. Does it's job of performing good monitoring for network devices specially. Helpful in quick identification of issues and bottlenecks and help reduce MTTR
cons: Certain features doesn't help you get down to the root-cause of the issues and investigation has to be done outside the tool.

review_title: Network monitor for everyone
description: This a great software for networks monitoring, which bring information on daily basis.
author: Nebojsa P.
pros: The web inteface that includes and well designed dashboard, shows all events that happen on the network and loggs them at the same time. Capable of reading advanced information from many devices.
cons: The intial configuration is time consuming, together with a need of many interventions.

review_title: Old Monitoring solution
description: Been using this to monitor our infrastructure​ but now we are looking for something new
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We purchased this many years ago to monitor our infrastructure and it started off well
cons: We found this solution very difficult to set up​ and their support was not much help.
